title: [lichen_l2.h:64] lichen_peer_add array parameter syntax gives false size safety
description:
Gap found: API signature review for header.

Why this was missed: Round 1 noted array decay but didn't flag header specifically.

The actual bug: The declaration at line 64:
  int lichen_peer_add(const uint8_t eui64[8], const uint8_t pubkey[32]);

The [8] and [32] in the parameter declarations provide no compile-time size checking - they decay to pointers. A caller can pass any buffer without warning:
  uint8_t bad[4];
  lichen_peer_add(bad, NULL);  // Compiles, causes UB

This is a known C limitation, but the header gives a false sense of type safety.

Options:
1. Change to pointer syntax to make decay explicit: (const uint8_t *eui64)
2. Add static analysis annotations: __attribute__((nonnull(1,2)))
3. Document clearly that size is not checked

The implementation does have runtime NULL checks but cannot verify buffer sizes.

Priority 3 - API clarity for security-sensitive interface.
close_reason: Duplicate of tvfm.7: BUILD_ASSERTs at lichen_l2.c:108-111 verify API array sizes match internal constants. Header documentation at lines 58-62 warns about C array decay. Fix already in place with tvfm.7 tracking reference in the code comment.
